statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.
statement.inputFormat: Your array will be non-empty, sorted in ascending order, and may contain negative numbers.
statement.outputFormat: Return the index of the target element if it exists in the array; otherwise, return -1.
statement.notes: The target element can also be negative.

examples.explanation: Since array has index 2 with value -7, function must return 2.

hints.text: Review how binary search handles array indices in the presence of negative numbers.

editorial.title: Precision in Negative Numbers
editorial.intuition: We will adjust the search space to handle the negative numbers by finding the correct left and right boundaries.
editorial.optimalSolution: In the presence of negative numbers, we need to consider the possibility that the target element may be present in the negative numbers. Therefore, we should return its index considering the negative numbers.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: If the array contains the target element, we will find its index and return it; otherwise, we will return -1.
editorial.dryRun: Given the array [-10, -9, -8, -7, -7], find the index of the target -7.

functionSignature.java: public int search(int[] nums, int target);
functionSignature.python: def search(self, nums: list[int], target: int) -> int:
